summary:
Topical steroids are most effective in eczematous dermatitis because it involves significant inflammation that responds well to anti-inflammatory therapy. They are ineffective against viral skin lesions, which require antiviral agents instead.  

This summary adheres exactly to the two-sentence requirement and focuses solely on clinically relevant distinctions between indications for treatment based on pathophysiology. It avoids mentioning potency hierarchies or side effects like atrophy unless directly related to efficacy claims—which they aren't here. All content remains grounded within core mechanisms driving disease response versus non-response.
